gaps.snippet: impl ScalingPoint { # [doc = " Speedup at each measured worker count vs the 1-worker sample (`t_1 / t_n`), paired with the"] # [doc = " worker count and the **ideal-linear** reference (`== workers`) for comparison. `None` when"] # [doc = " this point has no measured samples (skip/unmeasurable/empty)."] # [must_use] pub fn speedups (& self) -> Option < Vec < (usize , f64 , f64) > > { let ScalingOutcome :: Measured (samples) = & self . outcome else { return None ; } ; let base = samples . iter () . find (| s | s . workers == 1) ? . ns_per_job ; if base <= 0.0 { return None ; } Some (samples . iter () . map (| s | { let speedup = if s . ns_per_job > 0.0 { base / s . ns_per_job } else { 0.0 } ; # [allow (clippy :: cast_precision_loss)] let ideal = s . workers as f64 ; (s . workers , speedup , ideal) }) . collect () ,) } # [doc = " A coarse **Amdahl serial-fraction** estimate from the 1-worker and highest-worker-count"] # [doc = " samples: solving `speedup = 1 / (s + (1-s)/n)` for `s` at the max measured `n`. `Empirical`"] # [doc = " (fit from these two measured points, not a proof) — `None` when there are fewer than two"] # [doc = " distinct worker-count samples or the fit is degenerate (speedup <= 0 or >= n, both of which"] # [doc = " would imply a nonsensical negative/undefined serial fraction)."] # [must_use] pub fn amdahl_serial_fraction (& self) -> Option < f64 > { let sp = self . speedups () ? ; let (_ , s1 , _) = * sp . iter () . find (| (w , _ , _) | * w == 1) ? ; let (n , sn , _) = * sp . iter () . max_by_key (| (w , _ , _) | * w) ? ; if n <= 1 || s1 <= 0.0 || sn <= 0.0 { return None ; } # [allow (clippy :: cast_precision_loss)] let n = n as f64 ; let raw = (n / sn - 1.0) / (n - 1.0) ; Some (raw . clamp (0.0 , 1.0)) } }

gaps.snippet: # [doc = " Build a fresh [`Node`] + [`Engines`] and run `backend` on `case` once — the unit of work fanned"] # [doc = " out across the [`Scheduler`]'s worker pool. (o , Outcome :: Value (_))) { any_value = true ; } # [allow (clippy :: cast_precision_loss)] let batch_ns = elapsed . as_nanos () as f64 ; best = best . min (batch_ns) ; } if ! any_value { return None ; } # [allow (clippy :: cast_precision_loss)] let ns_per_job = best / f64 :: from (batch_size . max (1)) ; Some (ScalingSample { workers , batch_ns : best , ns_per_job , }) }
